title: Lovely Chewy Spanish Lentil Tagine
description: A kid-friendly Spanish dish featuring chewy lentils and a pop of blue color, made in a traditional tajine clay vessel and spherified for a fun twist, this recipe is also FODMAP friendly.
instructions: Rinse the lentils and soak them in water for 8 hours to ferment.
Heat the olive oil in the tajine clay vessel over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the soaked lentils, water, salt, and black pepper to the vessel and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes or until the lentils are tender.
Stir in the blue food coloring and sodium alginate.
Using a syringe, drop the lentil mixture into a bath of water mixed with calcium chloride to spherify.
Serve the spherified lentils hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
